office_does: The auditor administers elections and voter registration, records property documents, licenses vehicles and marriages, and performs county financial and payroll functions.
race_blurb: In this open race, Callie Gee and Dale Whitaker foreground accounting credentials, while Michael Cathcart emphasizes elected management, oversight, and open-government work.

candidates.summary: A public accountant, Gee emphasizes election access, vote-by-mail protection, transparent reporting, and private-sector financial management.
highlights: Works as a public accountant and has accounting and finance degrees [S1][S3]
Supports vote-by-mail protection, broad participation, and independent election oversight [S1][S2]
Promises transparent and fiscally responsible administration [S1]

spending.basis: She centers financial reporting, compliance, transparency, and fiscal responsibility.

experience.basis: She has professional accounting experience but no prior county-office management record.

candidates.summary: An enrolled agent and tax-practice owner, Whitaker presents the auditor primarily as an accounting and fraud-detection office.
highlights: Reports 18 years of accounting experience and prior corporate CFO work [S1][S3]
Proposes readable statements, modernized services, and close stewardship of public funds [S1][S2]
Emphasizes election security rather than an access-oriented program [S1]

spending.basis: His platform centers accounting controls, fraud detection, and close stewardship of existing funds.

experience.basis: He has extensive accounting and CFO experience but no county auditor management record.

candidates.summary: A Spokane city councilmember, Cathcart emphasizes his elected budget oversight, regional-board service, fraud prevention, and open-government record.
highlights: Has served on the Spokane City Council since 2020 with finance and regional-board roles [S1][S3]
Promises financial scrutiny, fraud prevention, election integrity, and public access to facts [S1][S2]
Points to budget oversight and open-government work as his relevant record [S1]

spending.basis: He repeatedly frames the office around scrutiny, fraud prevention, oversight, and public accountability.

experience.basis: He has the field's strongest elected and intergovernmental management record, though not direct auditor service.
